title: allium cepa var. viviparum
intro: Allium cepa var. viviparum, also known as viviparous garlic, is a rare and unique variety of garlic that produces small, green garlic cloves or "babies" on the end of its underground bulbs, allowing it to reproduce without going dormant. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal agent, which can help to combat infections and promote overall health. The health benefits of Allium cepa var. viviparum include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, making it a valuable addition to a holistic health regimen. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide a range of health benefits.
